name: ma_reversion_20d_rank
hypothesis: Stocks stretched far above their 20 day moving average tend to mean revert over the next short holding window when the move is not confirmed by broader liquidity.
expected_effect: Prefer lower recent deviation from the moving average after ranking the cross section.
novelty_note: Simple mean reversion baseline used to verify the factor_spec compiler path.

name: liquidity_shock_reversal_5_20d
hypothesis: A short-term price drop with elevated Amihud-style illiquidity often reflects temporary pressure and can rebound after the pressure clears.
expected_effect: Prefer names with negative recent return and elevated liquidity shock after cross-sectional ranking.
novelty_note: Combines reversal and liquidity pressure so it is not only another price-only mean reversion factor.
